brand.title: Allocation Studio
brand.tagline: AI-assisted stock distribution for global retail
brand.demo: Demo project · Not affiliated with On AG

info.stockHealth: % of SKU-store pairs with healthy cover (2–8 weeks). Below 60% typically means too many outliers — too thin or too deep.
info.sellThrough: Last week's sales ÷ (sales + remaining stock). Higher = stock is moving faster. Below 2% often signals slow-moving SKUs or overstock.
info.stockOuts: SKU-locations that ran to zero in the last week while demand was still positive. Each one is a lost sale today and a credibility issue with the store team.
info.weeklyRevenue: Units sold last completed week × list price. Use the vs LY pill to check whether growth is organic or driven by campaigns.
info.weeklyMargin: Units × (price − COGS). Margin is what you actually defend in the allocation meeting, not revenue.
info.inTransit: Units already committed on open POs (supplier → DC and DC → store). Always check this before approving more transfers.
info.forecastMape: Mean Absolute Percent Error over the last 8 weeks. Under 12% is solid; above 20% means forecasts are driving noise into decisions.
info.missedRevenue: Estimated lost sales from full stock-outs plus size gaps, valued at list price. The number this role is hired to reduce.
info.imbalanceCost: Weekly carrying cost of the excess units sitting at over-covered stores while others run thin. Redistributing cuts this directly.
info.salesByRegion: Weekly units sold per region, last 26 weeks. Look for divergence — a regional spike vs others usually maps to a campaign week.
info.stockByRegion: Average weeks of cover across SKUs per region. Under 2w is thin; over 10w means capital is sitting idle.
info.topMovers: SKUs with the highest revenue last completed week. Protect their size curve first — they are where the sell-through comes from.
info.atRisk: SKU-locations with less than 2 weeks of cover and positive demand. Replenish or redirect in the next cycle.
info.missedOpportunities: Top stock-outs and size gaps by lost revenue. Size-gap tags show which exact sizes went to zero.
info.imbalances: SKUs where one store has thin cover and another is long — the lowest-cover vs highest-cover pairing per SKU, with the carrying cost.
info.allocation: Recommendations triggered by live demand signals (velocity, upcoming campaign, size gap), not by the pre-season plan. Each one is monetised before approval.
info.sizesHeatmap: Rows = stores, columns = sizes. Red = stock-out with demand, amber = thin size cover, green = healthy. Top bar shows expected demand share per size.
info.forecastChart: Solid line = actual sales, dashed line = forecast, shaded band = 80% confidence, dotted grey = last year, orange bars = marketing spend (right axis).
info.risks: Risks tab: SKU-locations predicted to stock out in 2/4/6 weeks with revenue at risk. Opportunities tab: overstock pockets that can fund redistributions.
info.categories: Performance by product category — the rollup you bring into the Merchandise Planning meeting.

about.title: About this demo
about.subtitle: A portfolio project by Gemma Garcia de la Fuente for the Senior Retail Allocator role at On
about.demoIntro: Everything on this site is synthetic data rendered from deterministic seeds. It is a self-initiated prototype of how a modern allocation console could feel — combining demand-led replenishment, missed-sales detection, size performance and stock-imbalance analysis. Not affiliated with On AG.
about.hobbiesCopy: When I'm not building allocation tools, you'll find me on ski slopes through winter, on mountain trails through summer, and most weekends patiently teaching our recently-adopted pup that the Limmat riverside is for walking — she firmly disagrees with the ban on sniffing every flower along the way.
